channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Piles of filth, 26th April
item.description:
Dumping of bags of rubbish on the pavement directly opposite the primary school. Why can't the council crack down on the residents who consistently turn Maple Road into the area's filthiest street? How can the council possibly think that primary school children and responsible residents deserve to endure this constant mess? It's a shameful public health failure.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 160, Maple Road, Penge, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dumped bike, 26th April
item.description:
Left in field

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Water Lane, North Cray, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Abandoned car on Balcorne Street, 26th April
item.description:
Car has been there unmoved for several weeks - dirty inside and out, accumulating advertising flyers and evidence of discarded food containers that may attract vermin.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Balcorne Street, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Fences blocking foot path., 26th April
item.description:
The football club have blocked the paths and erected metal fences chaining them to the bushes, forcing the public to walk along the golf course path which is dangerous, and pass through a thorn bush. The fences are blocking public right of way and are way over the top. Could you please give notice for them to move the fences from the public foot paths. Regards, Matt.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Tanners Close, Crayford, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Broken (missing) drain cover, 26th April
item.description:
This is difficult to see in the dark, so potentially very dangerous for pedestrians crossing the road. It’s been like this for a while.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Cator Street, Old Kent Road, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Outside John Barker Court against a tree, 26th April
item.description:
Bed/mattress has been here for several weeks despite previously being reported by my husband a couple of weeks ago

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Brondesbury Park, Kilburn, London
Report on FixMyStreet
item.category: Fly-tip Medium - Up to medium size van load (Estates)

item.title: Graffiti on traffic signal pole, 26th April
item.description:
Red graffiti on a traffic signal pole.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Brixton Road, Oval, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dog fouling spread across pavement outside 38 and 40 Marlow Road, 26th April
item.description:
Yet again someone hasn’t bothered to clean up after their dog and has left it across drives and the pavement. The visually impaired should not have to experience this. Imagine how much money you could rake in if you had cameras to fine the culprits, particularly because Marlow Road and its surrounding roads has a prolific dog fouling problem, and it surely cannot be multiple disrespectful people (it is more likely to be one or two). I know it’s not the council’s fault, but perhaps more bins would help.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Marlow Road, Anerley, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Multiple potholes, 26th April
item.description:
This road is dangerous. You have to allow down in order not damage your car. Cars fully around the bend and a car had to avoid going into the back of me as they were going to fast. The road is dangerous and not all cars can drive over these at the normal speed. The pot holes damage my car every time I have to drive over them. Can always avoid them as you can drive around them as there are so many. The roads in Bromley are disgraceful and the repairs are awful.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 281, Leesons Hill, St Paul's Cray, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dangerous junction due to poor layout of road markings, poor signage and hazardous size of too large yellow box making passage across the yellow box in Green Lane totally impracticable for over 90% of the time., 26th April
item.description:
Dangerous junction due to poor layout of road markings, poor signage and hazardous size of too large yellow box making passage across the yellow box in Green Lane totally impracticable for over 90% of the time.
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Green Lane, Eltham
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Abandoned car VIN WOLGM67C891052651, 26th April
item.description:
This car has been here for several weeks. Reported to council but no action has been taken.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Kangley Bridge Road, Perry Vale, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Another overflowing bin, 26th April
item.description:
The bins on Marlow Road are atrocious - there are seemingly only two for the WHOLE road quite close to one another, which causes them to get overfilled and then spill out onto the street (once the foxes can access the overflow). It spills across the street and into people’s gardens. Think about how the visually impaired experience this kind of mess. More bins are needed, and more regular cleaning is required. I am sure this exacerbates the dog fouling issue because there’s nowhere to put dog poo if the nearest bin is overflowing (I know what the sensible, next best option is, but evidently other people don’t).
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Marlow Road, Anerley, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Swing Seat Broken, 26th April
item.description:
The rubber swing seat has broken away exposing the metal bracket. It is sharp and could cause injury. Please replace.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Seymour Villas, Anerley, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Over flowing bins, 26th April
item.description:
The bin on the lamp post on kentish way next to Harwood Avenue and the bin at the bottom of the steps near queens gardens were both full with rubbish all over the floor. These are really busy areas and the bins are always full. The situation. Is not helped with a resident of Harwood Avenue and sime residents of the halfway house in queens road continually putting their household rubbish in them. I am getting tired of lhaving to itter pick the areas. Please can something be done about this.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Plaistow, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Open manhole cover, 26th April
item.description:
Open manhole cover on pathway
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Beckenham Place Park, Beckenham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Children littering, 26th April
item.description:
The parents of kids in the council side of Clift house allow their kids to litter (crisp bags, paper towels, bottles) in the common area courtyard.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Colville Street, Hackney, London

item.title: Abandoned bicycle, 26th April
item.description:
Abandoned bicycle lying on the pavement near bike stand and Thames Water pillar. Wheel is bent out of shape and is blocking the other stands.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Kennington Park Road, Oval, Greater London
